name: PathDuplitron

consumes.minimum: 1 active composition + 1 selected layer with a Position property that has at least 1 selected keyframe
consumes.description: Reads app.project.activeItem (must be a CompItem), then comp.selectedLayers[0], then layer.property('Position'), and finally positionProp.selectedKeys. The Copy path also enumerates all position keyframes (numKeys) to write shape-path vertices. The Paste path only needs the same preconditions to verify before issuing an executeCommand(20).

controls.name: Copy
controls.role: copies position keyframe tangent data to clipboard via a temporary shape layer intermediate

controls.name: Paste
controls.role: pastes previously copied tangent data onto the selected position keyframes via AE paste command

functions: validate that the active item is a CompItem and that a layer is selected before acting
guard against layers without a Position property or with no keyframes selected
create a temporary shape layer and write position keyframe values as shape-path vertices via setValueAtTime for each keyframe
execute AE internal Copy command (id 10313) to copy the shape path with its tangent data to the clipboard
remove the temporary shape layer after the copy
execute AE internal Paste command (id 20) to apply previously copied tangent data to the selected position keyframes

scriptSim.scenario: success – copy position keyframe tangents via shape layer intermediate
scriptSim.inputs: Composition 'Motion Comp' (1920×1080, 24 fps, 10 s) with AV layer 'Animated Ball' selected; Position has 3 keyframes at t=0 ([200,540]), t=2 ([960,200]), t=4 ([1720,540]); first two keyframes selected

operations.target: shapeLayer/Contents/ADBE Vector Shape - Group/ADBE Vector Shape
shapeLayer/Contents/ADBE Vector Shape - Group/ADBE Vector Shape
shapeLayer/Contents/ADBE Vector Shape - Group/ADBE Vector Shape

notes: The panel opens as a floating 'palette' Window using a ScriptUI resource string; it remains open for repeated copy/paste operations within the same AE session.
Copy stores tangent data in the AE clipboard via executeCommand(10313); the clipboard is session-scoped and cleared by a new Copy or by closing AE.
The shape-layer intermediate (addShape) is created and immediately destroyed within the same Copy invocation; it is never visible to the user for more than a single frame.
setValueAtTime writes ALL position keyframes (positionProp.numKeys), not just the selected subset — the selected keyframes only gate whether the Copy runs at all.

Paste is a thin wrapper around executeCommand(20); it requires that a prior Copy (or any AE clipboard content) is available; no tangent merging is done in script — AE's native paste handles the transfer.
No beginUndoGroup/endUndoGroup wrapping is present; each operation is undoable individually (or not at all for executeCommand).

The script is compatible with both 2D and 3D Position properties; 3D layers expose keyValue as [x,y,z] but the script only reads indices 0 and 1, so Z is silently dropped into the 2D shape path vertex.
